epigraph.text: The first shall be last, and the last first.
epigraph.quote: Rank-based tests are remarkably efficient even when normality holds.
epigraph.quoteAuthor: Henry B. Mann \& Donald R. Whitney

content.text:
The Mann-Whitney U test (also called Wilcoxon rank-sum test) compares 
two independent groups without assuming normality.

content.title: Mann-Whitney U Test
content.content:
To test $H_0$: the two populations have the same distribution:

1. Combine all observations and rank them 1 to $n_1 + n_2$

2. Sum the ranks for each group: $R_1$ and $R_2$

3. Calculate $U_1 = n_1 n_2 + \frac{n_1(n_1+1)}{2} - R_1$

4. Compare to critical values or use normal approximation for 
 large samples

content.text:
$U$ measures how often observations from one group precede observations 
from the other. If the groups have identical distributions, $U$ should 
be close to $n_1 n_2 / 2$.

For large samples:
\[
 Z = \frac{U - n_1 n_2/2}{\sqrt{n_1 n_2 (n_1 + n_2 + 1)/12}} \approx \Normal(0,1)
\]
